title: Zeroization doc overstates AWS SSM guarantee
description:
Trigger: user reads the new # Zeroization doc section and treats AWS SSM as safe for zeroization-sensitive threat models.

The new doc says 'All other backends that do not use serde_json internally (env, file, AWS SSM) provide the zeroization guarantee.' This claim is false for AWS SSM.

The AWS SDK for Rust (aws-sdk-ssm) uses smithy-generated code that deserializes the HTTP response via serde. The secret value travels through non-Zeroizing String buffers inside the SDK before surfacing as p.value: Option<String>. The fact that our vault code calls serde_json functions directly while our SSM code does not does not mean SSM's internal path is Zeroizing — the SDK's own deserialization has the same gap.

The correct characterization: env and file have the guarantee. SSM, SM, and vault do not, because their SDKs all deserialize over the wire using non-Zeroizing buffers. The doc should either: (a) narrow the guarantee claim to env+file only, or (b) state that no network backend can claim full zeroization because the SDK layer is out of scope.
